alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hierarchical fuzzy inference tree (HFIT) produces a natural hierarchical structure that combines several low-dimensional fuzzy inference systems (FISs) for high approximation accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hierarchical fuzzy inference tree (HFIT) produces a natural hierarchical structure that accommodates simplicity by combining several low-dimensional fuzzy inference systems.
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the HFIT hierarchical structure; the publication reframes the emphasis toward simplicity (removing explicit accuracy phrasing), so the claim is clarified and slightly narrowed with tempered emphasis on accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The HFIT provides automatic feature selection by using multiobjective genetic programming (MOGP) for structural optimization, accepting only relevant inputs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The HFIT offers automatic feature selection by using multiobjective genetic programming for structural optimization.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim about automatic feature selection via MOGP with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The HFIT was found to provide less complex and highly accurate models compared to most other fuzzy inference system methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The HFIT provided less complex and highly accurate models compared to most other fuzzy inference system methods.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated verbatim in the publication; no substantive change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The HFIT demonstrates high diversity among the rules generated due to the heterogeneous nature of its nodes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The HFIT leads to a high diversity among the rules generated due to the heterogeneous nature of its nodes.
alignments.rationale: Both texts assert the same conclusion about rule diversity attributable to node heterogeneity with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many kernel clustering criteria have density biases that explain significant artifacts observed in practice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many kernel clustering criteria have density biases that explain significant artifacts observed in practice.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion identically; no change in meaning or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide conditions and formally prove the density mode isolation bias in kernel K-means for a common class of kernels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The density mode isolation bias in kernel K-means is formally proven under specific conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same formal result (proof under specified conditions); phrasing is slightly different but the claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Density equalization can be implicitly achieved using either locally adaptive weights or locally adaptive kernels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Density equalization can be implicitly achieved using locally adaptive weights or locally adaptive kernels.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this mechanism for achieving density equalization with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Density equalization makes many popular kernel clustering objectives equivalent.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Density equalization makes many popular kernel clustering objectives equivalent.
alignments.rationale: The claim that density equalization yields equivalence across objectives is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Theoretical understanding of kernel clustering limitations and their principled solutions will be important for a broad spectrum of data analysis applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Theoretical understanding of kernel clustering limitations and solutions is important for various data analysis applications.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the forward-looking/importance claim with slightly different wording; intent and confidence remain similar.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Pose-aware Multi-shot Matching (PaMM) framework robustly estimates people's poses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed pose-aware multi-shot matching framework robustly estimates individual poses and efficiently performs multi-shot matching based on pose information.
alignments.rationale: The publication merges pose estimation and matching efficiency into a single contribution statement; the pose-estimation claim is retained but presented together with matching efficiency (a clarification/merging).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods efficiently conduct multi-shot matching based on pose information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed pose-aware multi-shot matching framework robustly estimates individual poses and efficiently performs multi-shot matching based on pose information.
alignments.rationale: The publication combines the efficiency/matching claim with the pose-estimation claim into one consolidated contribution; the content is preserved but presented jointly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results show that the proposed methods outperform state-of-the-art methods in person re-identification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed methods outperform the current state-of-the-art methods in person re-identification.
alignments.rationale: The experimental result is restated directly in the publication with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods are promising for person re-identification from diverse viewpoints and pose variances.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods are promising for accomplishing person re-identification under diverse viewpoints and pose variances.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this concluding statement about promise under varied viewpoints/poses with equivalent phrasing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed approach successfully solved the image captcha of China's railroad system with 77% accuracy in 2 seconds on average.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed approach successfully solved the image captcha of China's railroad system with 77% accuracy in 2 seconds on average.
alignments.rationale: The result (accuracy and runtime) is repeated exactly in the publication; no change in meaning or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach is based on the principle of suspicious coincidence and demonstrates the power of unsupervised association learning techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach is based on the principle of suspicious coincidence, which suggests that the brain builds a statistical model of the world by learning associations between co-occurring events.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the conceptual framing of 'suspicious coincidence' with an explanatory analogy; the core claim about the principle and unsupervised association learning is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An association graph was constructed from 2.6 million captchas, linking over 6 million vertices based on co-occurrence information and feature similarity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An association graph was constructed from 2.6 million captchas, linking over 6 million vertices based on co-occurrence information and feature similarity.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the construction and scale of the association graph identically; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No pre-trained deep convolutional neural networks were available for recognizing the Chinese phrases or the presented images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system was trained without teaching labels for the Chinese phrases or the images, addressing significant challenges in the captcha-solving process.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the limitation as training without labels (a broader, stronger statement) and elevates the certainty about this challenge, amplifying the confidence compared to the preprint's more tentative limitation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed sampling method provides a highly accurate approximation of the full graph needed for multi-structural model fitting problems in computer vision.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed sampling method provides a highly accurate approximation of the full graph necessary for solving multi-structural model fitting problems in computer vision.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this contribution with equivalent meaning, scope, and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The usefulness of a graph for segmentation improves as the distribution of hypotheses approaches the distribution of actual parameters for the given data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The usefulness of a graph for segmentation improves as the distribution of the hypotheses used to build the graph approaches the distribution of the actual parameters for the given data.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this conclusion about hypothesis distribution and graph usefulness with only minor phrasing changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental analysis demonstrates that the proposed method is both accurate and computationally efficient compared to state-of-the-art robust multi-model fitting techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental analysis demonstrates that the proposed method is both accurate and computationally efficient compared to state-of-the-art robust multi-model fitting techniques.
alignments.rationale: The experimental result is carried over directly into the publication with the same claims about accuracy and efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper presents a reliable, bias-correcting score that can be efficiently computed to measure the strength of functional dependencies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper presents a reliable, bias-correcting score that can be efficiently computed to measure the strength of functional dependencies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is textually and semantically identical to the preprint claim, so there is no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An effective optimistic estimator of the score is introduced, allowing for the mining of approximate functional dependencies from data with guarantees of optimality. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An effective optimistic estimator of the score allows for mining approximate functional dependencies from data with guarantees of optimality. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim with equivalent wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Empirical evaluation demonstrates that the derived score achieves a good bias for variance trade-off and discovers meaningful dependencies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Empirical evaluation demonstrates that the derived score achieves a good bias for variance trade-off and discovers meaningful dependencies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical result is restated verbatim in the publication; no substantive change is present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method remains reliable in the face of data sparsity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method remains reliable in the face of data sparsity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about robustness to data sparsity is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithms display competitive performance on both synthetic and real data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithms display competitive performance on both synthetic and real data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is identical in content and emphasis between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We establish r-linear convergence for the iterative solution of the local dual minimization problem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We establish r-linear convergence for the iterative solution of the local dual minimization problem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The convergence result is carried over verbatim and unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose distributed solutions to the problem of Robust Subspace Recovery (RSR) without transferring data between nodes. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose distributed solutions to the problem of Robust Subspace Recovery (RSR) in an ad hoc network without a central processor. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the distributed-solution claim but specifies an ad hoc network and absence of a central processor, which clarifies and narrows the original, more general phrasing.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Consensus Based Gradient method is applied to the Geometric Median Subspace algorithm for RSR. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This specific methodological application is not present in the preprint and appears as a new publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The activation features trained without supervision are superior to descriptors of state-of-the-art writer identification methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The activation features trained without supervision are superior to descriptors of state-of-the-art writer identification methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about unsupervised activation features is unchanged and repeated in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We achieve comparable results in handwriting classification using the ICFHR16 competition dataset on historical Latin script types. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieve comparable results in handwriting classification using the ICFHR16 competition dataset on historical Latin script types. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same comparative performance claim on the ICFHR16 dataset.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Traditional methods are often better than or equivalent to deep learning methods when annotated training data is not available. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Traditional methods are often better than or equivalent to deep learning methods when annotated training data is not available. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about traditional vs. deep-learning methods under scarce annotations is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed fully automatic pipeline achieved accurate delineations of cardiac anatomy with a mean Dice score of 89%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed fully automatic pipeline achieved a mean Dice score of 89% for cardiac anatomy delineation and 79% for atrial scarring segmentation compared to manual ground truth segmentations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same 89% Dice result for cardiac anatomy delineation; the claim is preserved though Q0 bundles multiple results together.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The atrial scarring segmentation showed a mean Dice score of 79% compared to the established ground truth from manual segmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed fully automatic pipeline achieved a mean Dice score of 89% for cardiac anatomy delineation and 79% for atrial scarring segmentation compared to manual ground truth segmentations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same 79% Dice for atrial scarring; the single Q0 claim aggregates the preprint's two numeric results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The innovative pipeline demonstrated comparable results to previously studied methods with manual interventions, but was computed fully automatically. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly claim comparability to previously studied (manually intervened) methods; instead it reports metrics, so the comparative statement appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed segmentation methods allow LGE MRI to be used as an objective assessment tool for localisation, visualisation and quantification of atrial scarring. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The innovative pipeline allows LGE MRI to be used as an objective assessment tool for localization, visualization, and quantitation of atrial scarring. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved in the publication but assigned a lower certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered while content remains the same.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The automatic analysis obtained 88% segmentation accuracy, with 90% sensitivity and 79% specificity when compared to the ground truth. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This detailed accuracy/sensitivity/specificity breakdown is not present in the preprint and is introduced in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Receiver operating characteristic analysis of the automatic pipeline achieved an average area under the curve of 0.91. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The ROC/AUC result is a new quantitative evaluation reported only in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Graph-based methods successfully capture the underlying geometry of datasets in unsupervised and semi-supervised learning problems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Graph-based methods successfully solve unsupervised and semi-supervised learning problems by capturing the underlying geometry of datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core claim that graph-based methods capture dataset geometry to solve these problems; wording is slightly different but meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The bandwidth of any class indicator is closely related to the geometry of the dataset in the asymptotic limit. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the asymptotic limit, the bandwidth of any class indicator is closely related to the geometry of the dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The asymptotic bandwidth-geometry relationship is restated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The assumption of bandlimitedness of class indicator signals is theoretically justified through a sampling theoretic interpretation of graph-based semi-supervised classification. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The assumption of bandlimitedness of class indicator signals is theoretically justified through a sampling theoretic interpretation of graph-based semi-supervised classification. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries over the theoretical justification claim without change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The smoothness of class label information with respect to the graph is linked to the semi-supervised smoothness assumption and its variants. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This explicit linking of smoothness to the semi-supervised smoothness assumption is not stated in the preprint and appears as an additional interpretive claim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reinforcement learning can be used efficiently to train a spiking neural network to perform object recognition in natural images without using an external classifier. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reinforcement learning can be used efficiently to train a spiking neural network to perform object recognition in natural images without using an external classifier. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim about using reinforcement learning to train spiking networks for object recognition without an external classifier.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reward modulated STDP (R-STDP) approach extracted particularly discriminative visual features, outperforming classic unsupervised STDP on various image datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reward-modulated STDP (R-STDP) approach has extracted particularly discriminative visual features, outperforming classic unsupervised STDP on various image data sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about R-STDP extracting discriminative features and outperforming unsupervised STDP.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: R-STDP is suitable for online learning and can adapt to drastic changes such as label permutations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: R-STDP is suitable for online learning and can adapt to drastic changes such as label permutations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same statement about R-STDP's suitability for online learning and adaptation to label permutations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both feature extraction and classification were done with spikes, using at most one spike per neuron, making the network hardware friendly and energy efficient. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network is hardware friendly and energy efficient, performing both feature extraction and classification with at most one spike per neuron. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same contribution that both feature extraction and classification use at most one spike per neuron, emphasizing hardware friendliness and energy efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed semi-automated method, Indices-Net, achieves consistently low estimation error for LV wall thicknesses and areas of cavity and myocardium. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed semi-automated method, Indices-Net, achieves consistently low estimation error for LV wall thicknesses and areas of cavity and myocardium. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim for Indices-Net.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Indices-Net outperforms segmentation and two-phase direct volume-only methods with significant error reductions for wall thicknesses and areas. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Indices-Net outperforms segmentation methods and two-phase direct volume-only methods in terms of error reductions for wall thicknesses and areas. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates that Indices-Net outperforms segmentation and two-phase direct methods on the stated metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Joint learning of the deep convolution autoencoder and multiple output convolution neural network enhances the expressiveness of image representation and compatibility with indices regression. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Joint learning of the two networks in Indices-Net enhances the expressiveness of image representation and the compatibility between image representation and indices regression. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion about joint learning improving representation expressiveness and compatibility with regression.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method has great potential in clinical cardiac function assessment. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method has great potential in clinical cardiac function assessment. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim about clinical potential.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We define robustness in stable matching problems by introducing (a,b)-supermatches. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We define robustness in stable matching problems by introducing (a,b)-supermatches. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the contribution of introducing (a,b)-supermatches as the robustness definition.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Checking whether a given stable matching is a (1,b)-supermatch can be done in polynomial time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Checking whether a given stable matching is a (1,b)-supermatch can be done in polynomial time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the polynomial-time decidability result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our empirical evaluation shows that local search outperforms the other approaches in finding the most robust stable matching. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our empirical evaluation shows that local search outperforms the other approaches in finding the most robust stable matching. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical finding about local search outperforming alternatives appears identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced neural network represents sentences by composing their words according to induced binary parse trees. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models can learn to represent sentences by composing their words according to automatically induced binary trees without being shown a correct parse tree. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution that sentence representations are learned via automatically induced binary trees; wording emphasizes learning without provided parse trees but the core contribution is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model optimises both the composition function and the parser, eliminating the need for externally-provided parse trees. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models can learn to represent sentences by composing their words according to automatically induced binary trees without being shown a correct parse tree. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the optimization as models learning induced trees without being shown correct parse trees, clarifying the original claim about joint optimisation of composition and parser.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves better performance compared to various supervised Tree-LSTM architectures on a textual entailment task and a reverse dictionary task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach is competitive against similar models of comparable size and outperforms Tree-LSTMs that use trees produced by a parser. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes performance as being competitive overall while explicitly noting outperforming Tree-LSTMs that use parser-produced trees; this clarifies and slightly nuances the original claim about superior performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural Decomposition (ND) is a neural network technique for the analysis and extrapolation of time-series data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural decomposition (ND) is a neural network technique for the analysis and extrapolation of time-series data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same description of Neural Decomposition as a technique for time-series analysis and extrapolation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ND generalizes effectively on various time-series datasets, including the Mackey-Glass series and unemployment rates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ND generalizes effectively on various time-series data sets, including the Mackey-Glass series and unemployment rates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same empirical generalization claims on the listed datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ND outperforms popular time-series forecasting techniques such as LSTM, ARIMA, and SVR with a radial basis function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ND outperforms popular time-series forecasting techniques such as long short-term memory networks and ARIMA. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the claim that ND outperforms LSTM and ARIMA but omits SVR from the list, indicating a narrowed scope and a revision relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Amin, Heidari, and Kearns proved that tree networks can be learned by observing only the infected set of vertices of the contagion process under the independent cascade model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Amin, Heidari, and Kearns proved that tree networks can be learned by observing only the infected set of vertices of the contagion process under the independent cascade model.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simple modification of Amin et al.'s algorithm works on more general classes of networks, including networks with large girth and low path growth rate, and networks with bounded degree.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A simple modification of Amin et al.'s algorithm works on more general classes of networks, including networks with large girth and low path growth rate, and networks with bounded degree.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the result identically in scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings provide a partial theoretical explanation for Amin et al.'s experiments on sparse networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings provide a partial theoretical explanation for Amin et al.'s experiments on sparse networks.
alignments.rationale: The claim appears unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bayesian inference can be effectively used to extract large-scale modular structures from network data using the stochastic blockmodel.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Bayesian inference approach provides a methodologically correct answer to whether patterns of large-scale structure in networks can be supported by statistical evidence.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the claim to emphasize methodological correctness in supporting structure rather than the more general assertion of effective extraction, which narrows and clarifies the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nonparametric formulations of Bayesian inference prevent overfitting and enable model selection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework developed allows for the extraction of large-scale structure of networks while avoiding overfitting and underfitting.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the claim to explicitly state avoidance of both overfitting and underfitting and ties it to extracting structure, thereby clarifying and broadening the original preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inferring the stochastic blockmodel can predict missing and spurious links in networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inferring the stochastic blockmodel (SBM) can be used to predict missing and spurious links in networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim but presents it with higher certainty; the substantive content remains the same while confidence is stronger.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are fundamental limitations to the detectability of modular structures in networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The chapter sheds light on the fundamental limitations of the detectability of modular structures in networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the wording to indicate it 'sheds light' on limitations rather than simply stating them, which clarifies the scope without changing the substance or confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Abstracting events to a higher-level interpretation enables the discovery of more precise and comprehensible process models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Abstracting events to a higher-level interpretation enables the discovery of more precise and comprehensible process models.
alignments.rationale: The claim is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework can automatically abstract sensor-level events to their interpretation at the human activity level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework automatically abstracts sensor-level events to their interpretation at the human activity level.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The process models discovered after abstraction from real-life smart home event logs are more precise.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The process models discovered after abstraction improve on precision as well as on F-score.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit performance metric (F-score) in addition to precision, clarifying and broadening the evaluation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Process mining techniques can provide valuable insights into (un)healthy habits when applied to smart home data.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include the applied-health/insight claim present in the preprint, indicating it was omitted from the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: M_{CP} is able to rapidly learn good classifiers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm shows the ability to rapidly learn good classifiers.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim but reports it with slightly lower certainty (publication labels it medium rather than high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: M_{CP} shows superior generalization performance compared with conventional maximum margin methods using data augmentation methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: [Formula: see text] demonstrates superior generalization performance compared with conventional maximum margin methods using data augmentation methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the generalization-performance claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A proof of convergence for the M_{CP} algorithm is provided.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A proof of convergence is provided for the proposed algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same convergence proof claim with matching certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A polynomial bound on the number of iterations required for a desired tolerance in the objective function is established.
alignments.pub_claim_text: [Formula: see text] efficiently solves a quadratic semi-infinite programming problem to find the maximum margin solution.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes efficient solution of a related optimization formulation rather than explicitly stating a polynomial iteration bound, indicating the formal statement has been reframed and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed lifelong learning approach to unsupervised generative modeling incorporates newly observed distributions into a learned model without retaining past data or models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed lifelong learning approach to unsupervised generative modeling incorporates newly observed distributions into a learned model without retaining past data or models.
alignments.rationale: The claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of a novel cross-model regularizer inspired by a Bayesian update rule allows the student model to leverage information learned by the teacher model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of a novel cross-model regularizer inspired by a Bayesian update rule allows the student model to leverage information learned by the teacher model.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this contribution verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The regularizer reduces the effect of catastrophic interference that occurs when learning over sequences of distributions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The regularizer reduces the effect of catastrophic interference that occurs when learning over sequences of distributions.
alignments.rationale: The result is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's performance is validated on sequential variants of MNIST, FashionMNIST, PermutedMNIST, SVHN, and Celeb-A.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's performance is validated on sequential variants of MNIST, FashionMNIST, PermutedMNIST, SVHN, and Celeb-A.
alignments.rationale: The evaluation claim is restated identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model mitigates the effects of catastrophic interference faced by neural networks in sequential learning scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model mitigates the effects of catastrophic interference faced by neural networks in sequential learning scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this high-level result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A time series guided clustering algorithm can generate the most relevant meta-stable patterns from ab initio dynamic trajectories. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed time series guided clustering algorithm generates the most relevant meta-stable patterns directly from ab initio dynamic trajectories. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Wording and claim are essentially identical in contribution and certainty, so the claim is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An interpolation scheme based on known meta-stable patterns can accurately predict the ground and excited state properties of entire dynamics trajectories. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prediction with ensemble models (PEM) method does not require any training data beyond the clustering algorithm. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the method emphasizing that no additional training data are required; the predictive claim is retained but the focus shifts to the data/training aspect, so the claim is clarified rather than dropped.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The estimation error for both ground and excited state properties is very close, indicating similar accuracy in predictions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The estimation error for both ground and excited state properties is very close, indicating that one could predict these molecular properties with similar accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative/qualitative result and interpretation with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results may provide insights for constructing an excited state force field with compatible energy terms as traditional ones. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results may provide insights to construct molecular mechanism models with compatible energy terms as traditional force fields. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the tentative insight but generalizes from "excited state force field" to broader "molecular mechanism models," broadening the scope while keeping the low-certainty, suggestive nature.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A small backbone of fractures exists, where most flow and transport occurs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A small backbone of fractures exists, where most flow and transport occurs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical statement preserved between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Restricting the flowing fracture network to this backbone provides a significant reduction in the network's effective size. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Restricting the flowing fracture network to this backbone provides a significant reduction in the network’s effective size. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is restated verbatim and retained with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our alternative network reduction approach can reduce the network to approximately 20% of its original size. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictions from the new approach can reduce the network to approximately 20% of its original size while still generating breakthrough curves consistent with those of the original network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication combines the size-reduction claim with performance preservation (breakthrough curves), clarifying the practical implication while keeping the original numeric reduction claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictions from our method generate breakthrough curves consistent with those of the original network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictions from the new approach can reduce the network to approximately 20% of its original size while still generating breakthrough curves consistent with those of the original network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication merges this claim with the reduction-size claim to emphasize both accuracy of predictions and reduction effectiveness; the core content is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combined multi-view imagery from satellite to street-level can significantly benefit scene analysis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combined multiview imagery from satellite to street level can significantly benefit scene analysis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Essentially identical claim retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Direct matching of images on a per-pixel basis is often impossible due to varying viewpoints, scales, illumination, sensor modalities, and times of acquisition. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of overhead and street-level images presents challenges due to varying viewpoints, scales, illuminations, sensor modalities, and acquisition times. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the technical limitations with the same factors and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cross-fertilization between remote sensing, computer vision, and machine learning is valuable for optimizing the use of geographic data from Earth Observation sensors and ground imagery. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cross fertilization between remote sensing, computer vision, and machine learning is valuable for maximizing the use of geographic data from Earth observation sensors and ground imagery. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this interdisciplinary claim with similar wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating complementary data sources from remote sensing and ground imagery is expected to lead to major breakthroughs in Big GeoData. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating complementary data sources from remote sensing and ground imagery is expected to lead to major breakthroughs in Big GeoData. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this forward-looking conclusion with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A multi-dimensional approach to injury forecasting in professional soccer based on GPS measurements and machine learning is proposed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A multi-dimensional approach to injury forecasting in professional soccer based on GPS measurements and machine learning is proposed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in publication and preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The injury forecaster constructed using GPS tracking technology is both accurate and interpretable. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The injury forecaster constructed using GPS tracking technology is both accurate and interpretable. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result preserved verbatim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach provides a set of simple and practical rules for evaluating and interpreting the complex relations between injury risk and training performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach provides a set of simple and practical rules for evaluating and interpreting the complex relations between injury risk and training performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is unchanged in wording and intent in the published paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An unsupervised model using distributed representation of words and domain knowledge effectively extracts appropriate context from reference papers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An unsupervised model using distributed representation of words and domain knowledge effectively extracts appropriate context from reference papers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core result is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art in citation context extraction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art in citation context extraction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is preserved with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An effective contextualization method improves citation-based summarization of scientific articles. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An effective contextualization method improves citation-based summarization of scientific articles. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion linking contextualization to improved summarization is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The divide and conquer (D&C) method for symbolic regression can improve the convergence speed of genetic programming (GP) for large scale problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The D&C powered symbolic regression can help obtain the target function more quickly and reliably.
alignments.rationale: Both claims state the D&C approach speeds up obtaining the target function; the publication frames this more generally for symbolic regression (broader than GP) and adds reliability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The target function's separability is assessed using a new technique called the Bi-Correlation test (BiCT).
alignments.pub_claim_text: A separability pattern detecting technique, bi-correlation test (Bi-CT), is proposed to assist in the D&C process.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same BiCT separability detector to support D&C; wording and role are effectively the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: D&C powered GP has been tested on real-world applications and demonstrates a significant improvement in the speed of obtaining the target function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The D&C powered symbolic regression can help obtain the target function more quickly and reliably.
alignments.rationale: The preprint claims tested real-world improvements explicitly; the publication keeps a general claim of faster/more reliable recovery but omits the explicit real-world testing and strong performance wording, so the claim is weakened/narrowed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A divide and conquer (D&C) scheme is proposed to improve symbolic regression by dividing the search space into orthogonal sub-spaces.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an explicit characterization of the D&C scheme (division into orthogonal sub-spaces) that was not stated as a separate claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporation of prior knowledge about organ shape and location improves the performance of image analysis approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating prior knowledge about organ shape and location improves the performance of image analysis approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed training strategy incorporates anatomical prior knowledge into CNNs through a new regularisation model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed training strategy incorporates anatomical prior knowledge into CNNs through a new regularisation model.
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution described in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new framework encourages models to follow the global anatomical properties of the underlying anatomy via learned non-linear representations of the shape.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new framework encourages models to follow the global anatomical properties of the underlying anatomy via learnt non-linear representations of the shape.
alignments.rationale: Same result claim with matching wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach can be easily adapted to different analysis tasks and improve the prediction accuracy of state-of-the-art models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach can be easily adapted to different analysis tasks and improve the prediction accuracy of state-of-the-art models.
alignments.rationale: Same claim of adaptability and accuracy improvement in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learned deep models of 3D shapes can be interpreted and used as biomarkers for classification of cardiac pathologies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learnt deep models of 3-D shapes can be interpreted and used as biomarkers for classification of cardiac pathologies.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this interpretation/biomarker claim with the same confidence and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hybrid neural networks designed in this study demonstrate higher prediction accuracy than traditional models that use only original input and output.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network enhanced the last 1-2% of prediction accuracy while eliminating questionable predictions.
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserts a general higher accuracy; the publication narrows this to a modest (1-2%) improvement and focuses on eliminating questionable predictions, so the claim is weakened and made more specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of auxiliary inputs, or indicators, enhances the logical learning process by providing hints that suggest logical outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network design using auxiliary inputs can mimic human deductive reasoning.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert that auxiliary inputs enable reasoning-like/logical behavior; the publication frames this as mimicking deductive reasoning, consistent with the preprint's claim about enhancing logical learning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The logical learning process can effectively manage unknown unknowns by utilizing all available knowledge for learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include a corresponding claim about managing 'unknown unknowns' or using all available knowledge, so this preprint claim appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The design and implementation of indicators are essential for advancing artificial intelligence in logical learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Different sets of auxiliary inputs can be designed to formulate various reasonings to explain predicted results without losing trustworthiness.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the importance of indicators into an actionable result about designing auxiliary inputs for varied reasoning/explanations; this clarifies and operationalizes the preprint's more general assertion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Future applications of the network with auxiliary inputs can optimize robotic learning in detection problems.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a forward-looking application (robotic learning) that is not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed RNN model reduces the number of parameters significantly by representing the weight parameters based on Tensor Train format.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed RNN model reduces the number of parameters significantly by representing the weight parameters based on Tensor Train format.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same contribution about parameter reduction via TT-format representation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TT-format representation is implemented for several RNN architectures, including simple RNN and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper implements the TT-format representation for several RNN architectures, including simple RNN and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU).
alignments.rationale: Implementation across several RNN architectures is preserved unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed RNNs with TT-format are able to preserve performance while reducing the number of RNN parameters up to 40 times smaller.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed RNNs with TT-format are able to preserve performance while reducing the number of RNN parameters up to 40 times smaller.
alignments.rationale: The result about preserving performance while achieving large parameter reductions is carried over identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) outperform traditional methods in various visual recognition tasks including Facial Expression Recognition (FER).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) outperform traditional methods in various visual recognition tasks including Facial Expression Recognition (FER).
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level result about DNN performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing methods for FER using DNN are not generalizable enough in practical applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing methods for FER using DNN are not generalizable enough in practical applications.
alignments.rationale: The limitation claim is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed 3D Convolutional Neural Network method for FER in videos outperforms state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed 3D Convolutional Neural Network method for FER in videos outperforms state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim for the proposed 3D CNN remains unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new network architecture extracts both spatial relations within facial images and temporal relations between different frames in the video.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new network architecture extracts both spatial relations within facial images and temporal relations between different frames in the video.
alignments.rationale: Same contribution preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Facial landmark points are used as inputs to the network, emphasizing the importance of facial components in generating facial expressions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Facial landmark points are used as inputs to the network, emphasizing the importance of facial components in generating facial expressions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study infers a new set of potential human traits based on unprompted language use from social media data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach suggests a value in new constructs of personality derived from everyday human language use.
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserts an inference of a new set of traits, while the publication softens this to a suggestion of value for such constructs, narrowing the claim from a direct inference to a more cautious conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The language-based trait construct predicts non-questionnaire-based outcomes better than traditional questionnaire-based traits.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our language-based trait construct is often more generalizable in that it often predicts non-questionnaire-based outcomes better than questionnaire-based traits.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same core claim but adds qualifiers (e.g., "often"), which clarifies and slightly tempers the original definitive statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The factors derived from language use remain nearly as stable as traditional personality factors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The factors derived from language use remain nearly as stable as traditional factors.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result with essentially identical wording and confidence about stability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach suggests a value in new constructs of personality derived from everyday human language use.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach suggests a value in new constructs of personality derived from everyday human language use.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint conclusion verbatim, with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most plausible model is not always the most predictive model for network structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The most plausible model is not always the most predictive model for network structures.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint claim directly with the same meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Improving predictive performance can lead to overfitting in both artificial and empirical settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Improvement in predictive performance can lead to overfitting in both artificial and empirical settings.
alignments.rationale: The claim appears unchanged in wording and intent; the publication affirms the same result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictive performance is generally higher when averaging over collections of models that are individually less plausible than when considering only the single most plausible model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Averaging over collections of models that are individually less plausible yields higher predictive performance than considering only the single most plausible model.
alignments.rationale: The content is the same, but the publication marks this claim with lower certainty (medium), indicating a slight tempering of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three neural network-based methods for facial affect estimation were proposed and evaluated.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper presents three neural network-based methods for facial affect estimation.
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same contribution of three neural-network methods; wording is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Deep Inception-ResNet method achieved a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.4 for valence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Deep Inception-ResNet method achieves a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.4 for valence.
alignments.rationale: The numerical RMSE claim for valence is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Deep Inception-ResNet method achieved a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.3 for arousal.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Deep Inception-ResNet method achieves a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.3 for arousal.
alignments.rationale: The numerical RMSE claim for arousal is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The corresponding Concordance Correlation Coefficient (CCC) rates for valence and arousal using the Deep Inception-ResNet method were 0.04 and 0.29, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Deep Inception-ResNet method has a Concordance Correlation Coefficient (CCC) of 0.04 for valence.
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the paired CCC statement into two separate claims (valence and arousal). This mapping links to the valence CCC; the arousal CCC is represented separately in the publication (Q4) but corresponds to the same preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing symbolic parsing algorithms can be entirely formulated over distributed representations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing symbolic parsing algorithms for context-free languages can be entirely formulated over distributed representations.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the domain (context-free languages), narrowing and clarifying the original broader claim about "existing symbolic parsing algorithms."

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The D-CYK algorithm is entirely defined over distributed representations and uses matrix multiplication on real number matrices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The distributed (D)-CYK algorithm is entirely defined over distributed representations and uses matrix multiplication on real number matrices.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the algorithmic definition with equivalent wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments show that the D-CYK algorithm approximates the original CYK algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Preliminary experiments show that D-CYK approximates the original CYK algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The publication qualifies the experimental result as "preliminary," which clarifies and weakens the strength of the original experimental claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ability to perform CYK on distributed representations opens the way to defining recurrent layers of CYK-informed neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ability to perform CYK entirely on distributed representations enables the definition of recurrent layer neural networks that can process general context-free languages.
alignments.rationale: The publication both clarifies and strengthens the downstream implication by explicitly stating these recurrent layers can process general context-free languages, broadening the scope and increasing asserted confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel algorithm simple-ReProCS provides the first guarantee for dynamic RPCA under weakened versions of standard RPCA assumptions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper provides the first guarantee for dynamic RPCA that holds under weakened versions of standard RPCA assumptions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simple-ReProCS achieves significantly improved outlier tolerance compared to existing RPCA or dynamic RPCA solutions by exploiting two simple extra assumptions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm, simple-ReProCS, achieves significantly improved outlier tolerance compared with all existing RPCA or dynamic RPCA solutions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simple-ReProCS is online, fast, and has near-optimal memory complexity after initialization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simple-ReProCS is online (after initialization), fast, and has near-optimal memory complexity.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance characteristics of the algorithm with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Temporal planning is a viable approach to quantum circuit compilation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Temporal planning is a viable approach to quantum circuit compilation.
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication text are identical in claim and strength, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of temporal planners can effectively address the problem of compiling quantum circuits for emerging quantum hardware.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of temporal planners can effectively address the problem of compiling quantum circuits for emerging quantum hardware.
alignments.rationale: The wording and asserted contribution are the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Compiling Quantum Alternating Operator Ansatz (QAOA) circuits presents greater potential for optimization due to their high number of commuting gates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Compiling Quantum Alternating Operator Ansatz (QAOA) circuits presents greater potential for optimization due to their high number of commuting gates.
alignments.rationale: The result about QAOA optimization potential is unchanged in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An automatic method for charge and mass identification of charged nuclear fragments has been developed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article presents a proof of concept for using an evolutionary strategy in the Δ E − E identification procedure on simulated data.
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserts a developed automatic method generally, whereas the publication frames the work as a proof-of-concept limited to simulated ΔE−E data, narrowing and tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm utilizes a generative model of DeltaE - E relation combined with a Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolutionary Strategy (CMA-ES).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm combines a generative model of Δ E − E relation with a Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolutionary Strategy (CMA-ES).
alignments.rationale: The description of the algorithmic combination is effectively identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CMA-ES is employed as a stochastic and derivative-free method to search the parameter space of the model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CMA-ES is employed as a stochastic and derivative-free method to search the parameter space of the model.
alignments.rationale: The role of CMA-ES is stated the same way in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for detecting adversarial image examples can be directly deployed into unmodified off-the-shelf DNN models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for detecting adversarial image examples can be directly deployed into unmodified off-the-shelf DNN models.
alignments.rationale: Claim about deployability into unmodified DNNs is unchanged verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The image entropy is employed as a metric to implement an adaptive noise reduction for different kinds of images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The image entropy is employed as a metric to implement an adaptive noise reduction for different kinds of images.
alignments.rationale: The methodological use of image entropy is stated identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The detection method can achieve a high overall F1 score of 96.39% when evaluated against more than 20,000 adversarial examples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The detection method can achieve a high overall F1 score of 96.39 percent when evaluated against more than 20,000 adversarial examples.
alignments.rationale: The reported evaluation result and dataset size are identical in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method effectively detects adversarial examples without referring to any prior knowledge of attacks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method effectively detects adversarial examples without referring to any prior knowledge of attacks.
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about attack-agnostic detection is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Visual scoring of Her2 is subjective and prone to inter-observer variability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is significant interobserver variability in visual scoring of HER2 by immunohistochemistry.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same observation about subjectivity and inter-observer variability in visual HER2 scoring.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated methods for Her2 scoring could outperform pathology experts in a contest dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automated methods for HER2 scoring can outperform pathology experts in scoring accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The preprint hedges performance (could, contest dataset) while the publication presents a firmer, more general statement (can outperform), broadening and strengthening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper presents a benchmark for comparing the performance of automated algorithms for scoring of Her2.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides a benchmark for comparing the performance of automated algorithms for HER2 scoring.
alignments.rationale: Both texts present the same contribution of providing a benchmark for automated HER2 scoring.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated algorithms have enormous potential in assisting pathologists with objective IHC scoring.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A more objective method for HER2 scoring is required due to the subjective nature of visual scoring.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasizes the potential of automated algorithms; the publication reframes this as a requirement for more objective methods, removing the explicit optimistic claim about algorithms' potential and tempering the original assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generative model trained with synthetic images reduces the workload of data collection and limitations of conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed generative model reduces the burden of collecting real training data by utilizing synthetic images rendered from 3D models.
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same contribution: using synthetic images to reduce real data collection burden.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed structure achieves state of the art accuracy of over 50% on the ShapeNet database.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The architecture achieves state-of-the-art accuracy of 50.5% on the ShapeNet database.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a more precise numeric value (50.5%) refining the preprint's "over 50%" statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-triplet cost function based on poses enables training a category classifier purely based on synthetic data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multi-triplet cost function based on poses enables effective training of a categorical classifier using only synthetic data.
alignments.rationale: The claim is substantively the same, but the publication tones down certainty to 'effective' (published certainty is medium vs preprint high), so confidence is marked as tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The coordinate training strategy with adaptive noises helps both sub-networks benefit from each other.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The coordinate training strategy with adaptive noise helps the two sub-networks benefit from each other and avoids inharmonious parameter tuning.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the claim by explicitly adding that the strategy avoids inharmonious parameter tuning, clarifying and slightly extending the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Exploiting vehicle traffic can facilitate security surveillance through effective camera placement strategies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using vehicle traffic patterns to strategically place cameras could potentially facilitate security surveillance.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same idea but softens the certainty (adds "could potentially"), so the claim is clarified and confidence is tempered while scope remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This article is the first effort to link vehicle traffic with security surveillance in smart cities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This article constitutes the first effort toward building the link between vehicle traffic and camera placement for better security surveillance.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same novelty claim with equivalent emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study aims to influence decision making regarding surveillance camera placement.
alignments.rationale: The published version does not include or echo the stated aim about influencing decision making, so this aim-style claim is effectively removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research could foster further studies on principled methods of security surveillance.
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the forward-looking statement about fostering further studies, indicating this concluding conjecture was removed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Deploying numerous cameras is a common approach to security surveillance in smart cities.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a general statement about common practice (deployment of numerous cameras) that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm effectively solves parabolic partial differential equations and backward stochastic differential equations in high dimensions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new algorithm effectively solves parabolic partial differential equations and backward stochastic differential equations in high dimensions.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same effectiveness claim for high-dimensional PDEs/BSDEs with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analogy between backward stochastic differential equations and reinforcement learning enhances the solution process by utilizing the gradient of the solution as the policy function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm utilizes an analogy between backward stochastic differential equations and reinforcement learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the analogy but drops the explicit framing that it 'enhances the solution' via using the gradient as policy, so the claim is presented in a more neutral/clarified form.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical results demonstrate the efficiency and accuracy of the proposed algorithms for several 100-dimensional nonlinear PDEs from physics and finance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical results demonstrate the efficiency and accuracy of the algorithm for 100-dimensional nonlinear PDEs.
