alignments.rationale: The publication presents a broader claim that the network can learn human-like problem solving but omits the preprint's specific necessity of sufficient hidden layers, changing the emphasis of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The centralisation technique, the symbolic association vectors, and the rule application records can reduce error rates of reasoning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Human-like rewriting bridges the gap between symbolic reasoning and biologically inspired machine learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the technical contributions as enabling a bridge between paradigms (a higher-level characterization) rather than enumerating error-rate reductions, which clarifies the contribution without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approaches used in this research have led to a 4.6% error rate of reasoning on a dataset of linear equations, differentials, and integrals.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Human-like rewriting shows satisfactory performance on the tasks of solving linear equations and computing derivations and indefinite integrals.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports satisfactory performance on the same tasks but omits the exact 4.6% error figure, thereby softening/specifying the result without changing task scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methodology can accelerate the optimization of material composition and processing by fitting data-driven models to experimental data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methodology can accelerate the optimization of material composition and processing by fitting data-driven models to experimental data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Text and meaning are effectively identical between preprint and publication, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This methodology allows scientists to test the most promising candidates earlier than traditional methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This methodology allows practitioners to test the most promising candidates earlier than traditional methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Only minor wording change (scientists→practitioners) with same substance and certainty, so unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The data-driven models used in the methodology incorporate uncertainty analysis to guide experimental proposals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The data-driven models used in the methodology incorporate uncertainty analysis to guide the selection of new experiments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same idea with slight wording change (guide experimental proposals → guide selection of new experiments) without altering meaning or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Over four materials science test cases, the methodology found the optimal candidate with three times fewer required measurements than random guessing on average. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Over four materials science test cases, the methodology found the optimal candidate with three times fewer required measurements than random guessing on average. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical quantitative result reported in both versions, so unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new model and algorithm perform an efficient Natural gradient descent for Multilayer Perceptrons. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new model and algorithm perform an efficient Natural gradient descent for multilayer perceptrons. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Claim is the same aside from minor orthographic differences; meaning and confidence unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The whitening process is extended to include both feed-forward and back-propagation phases. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The whitening process is extended to the back-propagation phase in addition to the feed-forward direction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Same technical contribution described with slightly different phrasing; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The efficacy of the Bidirectional whitened neural networks model is demonstrated through its application to handwritten character recognition data (MNIST data). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficacy of the Bidirectional Whitened Neural Networks model is demonstrated through its application to handwritten character recognition using MNIST data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same experimental demonstration reported with only minor capitalization/phrasing changes; claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We define the notion of a well-clusterable data set that combines the objective of the $k$-means clustering algorithm and the requirement for clusters to be separated by gaps. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The notion of a well-clusterable data set is defined by combining the objective of the k-means clustering algorithm and the requirement for clusters to be separated by gaps. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Definition is restated verbatim in the publication; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identify conditions under which the optimum of the $k$-means objective coincides with a clustering where the data is separated by predefined gaps. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Conditions are identified under which the optimum of the k-means objective coincides with a clustering where the data is separated by predefined gaps. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same result stated in publication with equivalent wording; unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novelty of our approach lies in the possibility of an a posteriori check to determine if the data set is well-clusterable after running $k$-means. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method allows for an a posteriori check to determine if a data set is well-clusterable after running k-means. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same a posteriori check claim but presents it more assertively (higher certainty), so the claim is clarified and confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: If $k$-means++ fails to identify a clustering that meets clusterability criteria, with high probability the data is not well-clusterable. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: If k-means++ fails to identify a clustering that meets clusterability criteria, it is highly probable that the data is not well-clusterable. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Substantively the same probabilistic conclusion, but the publication weakens the stated certainty from high to medium, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper overcomes the major obstacle of using known clusterability criteria, which reference the NP hard optimal clustering. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This claim about overcoming the NP-hard obstacle is present in the publication but has no counterpart in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed semantic information guided framework enhances target scatters while suppressing background clutters in SAR image formation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed semantic information guided generative framework enhances target scatters while suppressing background clutters in SAR image formation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result (minor addition of 'generative' adjective) without changing substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new semantics-specific regularizer was developed by exploiting the statistical properties of different semantic categories in a target scene SAR image. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new semantics-specific regularizer is developed to exploit the statistical properties of different semantic categories in target scene SAR images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Same contribution described with near-identical wording; unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed framework for SAR image formation compared to state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed framework for SAR image formation compared to state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical experimental claim in both versions; unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework introduces machine learning strategies to image formation, which can benefit subsequent decision-making tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework introduces machine learning strategies to image formation, which can benefit subsequent decision-making tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same concluding claim with same certainty; unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The interplay between connectivity and synchronization dynamics in biological systems often leads to functional clusters that do not coincide with anatomical modules. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interplay between connectivity and synchronization dynamics in biological systems often leads to functional clusters that do not coincide with anatomical modules. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical result stated in both preprint and publication; unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quest for consensus in social, socio-technical, and engineering systems favors the emergence of clusters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The quest for consensus in social, sociotechnical, and engineering systems favors the emergence of clusters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same claim with only minor hyphenation change; unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A method based on random walk dynamics can define the diffusion distance between any pair of units in a networked system. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A method based on random walk dynamics can define the diffusion distance between any pair of units in a networked system. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Content is the same but the publication raises the stated certainty from medium to high, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed metric allows for the exploitation of diffusion geometry to provide a unifying framework for understanding the relationship between metastable synchronization, consensus, and random search dynamics in complex networks. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed metric allows for the exploitation of diffusion geometry to provide a unifying framework for understanding the relationship between metastable synchronization, consensus, and random search dynamics in complex networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Substantively identical claim; only its categorization (contribution→conclusion) changed in the metadata, not the claim content or confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DNN-based text classifiers are prone to adversarial sample attacks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The adversarial samples generated by our method can successfully fool both state-of-the-art character-level and word-level DNN-based text classifiers. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The adversarial samples can be perturbed to any desirable classes without compromising their utilities. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced perturbation is difficult to be perceived. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduced perturbation is difficult to be perceived. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This descriptive result appears identically in the publication, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed architecture allows for adopting several classes of existing process discovery techniques in the context of event streams. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed architecture allows for adopting several classes of existing process discovery techniques in the context of event streams. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Learning process models from event streams poses challenges and opportunities, including the need to handle unlimited amounts of data using finite memory. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Segmentation-based PVS burden estimates correlate well with neuroradiological assessments, with a Spearman’s ρ of 0.74 and p < 0.001. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multiclass support vector machine for big data classification with a quantum all-pair approach can be implemented in logarithm runtime complexity on a quantum computer. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach exhibits exponential speed up in the quantum version compared to the classical multiclass support vector machine that can be implemented with polynomial run time complexity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The quantum approach for the all-pair multiclass classification problem exhibits exponential speedup compared to classical multiclass support vector machines. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new dataset of dense reference and distorted light fields along with corresponding quality scores has been collected. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new dataset of dense reference and distorted light fields has been collected along with corresponding quality scores scaled in perceptual units. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing image quality metrics provide good measures of light-field quality but require dense reference light-fields for optimal performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing image quality metrics provide good measures of light-field quality but require dense reference light-fields for optimal performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of existing quality metrics drops significantly when comparing two distorted light fields, indicating a need for new, light-field-specific metrics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of existing quality metrics drops significantly when comparing two distorted light fields, indicating the need for new, light-field-specific metrics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed CNN-based framework achieves 89.3% accuracy on the validation set of the NTU RGB+D dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficacy of the proposed method has been verified by achieving state-of-the-art results on the large RGB+D Dataset and small UTD-MHAD Dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the specific NTU validation accuracy as a broader state-of-the-art claim on the large RGB+D dataset (which corresponds to NTU), thus clarifying and broadening the original numerical result and presenting it more strongly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel skeleton transformer module automatically rearranges and selects important skeleton joints.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on joint distance maps and CNN encoding rather than mentioning a skeleton transformer that rearranges/selects joints, so this specific contribution is not represented.

alignments.rationale: The publication does not report PKU-MMD detection mAP or a window proposal network for untrimmed-action detection, so this specific result appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance on the PKU-MMD dataset surpasses the baseline by a large margin.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method effectively encodes spatio-temporal information of skeleton sequences into color texture images known as joint distance maps (JDMs).
alignments.rationale: The JDM representation is introduced in the publication but has no clear counterpart in the preprint claims provided.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks are employed to exploit discriminative features from the joint distance maps for human action and interaction recognition.
alignments.rationale: While the preprint mentions a CNN framework, the specific framing of CNNs applied to joint distance maps is a claim newly emphasized in the publication and not directly present in the listed preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithms D-SAMD and AD-SAMD provide bounds on convergence rates based on the number of nodes, network topology, and the ratio of data streaming to communication rates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithms D-SAMD and AD-SAMD have bounds on their convergence rates that depend on the number of nodes, network topology, and the ratio of data streaming to communication rates.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For sufficiently well-connected networks, distributed learning schemes can achieve order-optimum convergence even with small communication rates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distributed learning schemes can achieve order-optimum convergence in sufficiently well-connected networks even with small communication rates.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of accelerated methods significantly expands the regime for achieving order-optimum convergence compared to centralized settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of accelerated methods significantly increases the regime for achieving order-optimum convergence compared to centralized settings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the proposed algorithms is demonstrated through numerical experiments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of the proposed algorithms is demonstrated through numerical experiments.
alignments.rationale: An identical claim about empirical demonstration appears in both documents.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Sufficient conditions for order-optimum convergence of D-SAMD and AD-SAMD are established.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states sufficient conditions, a formalization that is not present among the listed preprint claims and thus appears as an added emphasis/detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed proximal-gradient algorithm allows agents to use uncoordinated step-sizes that are independent of network topologies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed proximal-gradient algorithm allows agents to use uncoordinated step-sizes that are independent of network topologies.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Linear convergence can be achieved for the special case without non-smooth terms under the strong convexity assumption.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Linear convergence can be achieved for the special case without nonsmooth terms under the strong convexity assumption.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convergence rate of the new algorithm is as good as one of the two convergence rates that match the typical rates for general gradient descent and consensus averaging.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The convergence rate of the new algorithm is as good as one of the two convergence rates that match the typical rates for general gradient descent and consensus averaging.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm has advantages over the previous algorithm, PG-EXTRA, particularly in terms of step-size flexibility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A team's position in a competition's final ranking is significantly related to its typical performance, as described by a set of technical features extracted from the soccer data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A team’s position in a competition’s final ranking is significantly related to its typical performance, as described by a set of technical features extracted from the soccer data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel neural network architecture effectively captures hierarchical structures of man-made 3D objects of varying structural complexities.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims focus on shape correspondence and comparison rather than claiming the architecture captures hierarchical structures; no matching statement is present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned bidirectional mapping yields a generative model of plausible structures from which novel structures can be sampled.
alignments.rationale: The paper's published claims do not describe generative sampling from a learned mapping, so this preprint claim is not reflected in the publication.

alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not discuss shape synthesis or interpolation; emphasis shifted to correspondence and comparison, leaving this synthesis claim out.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed parsimonious random vector functional link network (pRVFLN) features an open structure paradigm that allows for automatic generation of its network structure based on the degree of nonlinearity and time-varying properties of the system being modeled. alignments.preprint_claim_text: pRVFLN introduces a non-parametric type of hidden node that reflects the real data distribution without being constrained by a specific shape of the cluster. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: pRVFLN introduces a non-parametric type of hidden node that reflects the real data distribution without being constrained by a specific shape of the cluster. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
